case_name: William McNae and Ronda McNae v. ARAG Insurance Company
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Legal Norm | Plaintiffs relied on a purported quotation from Local Civil Rule 1(c) claiming it "permits flexibility 'in the interest of justice'", but the rule contains no such language and the attribution was inaccurate. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiffs attributed the quotation "factual materials underlying an ERISA determination are discoverable" to Abatie v. Alta Health & Life Ins. Co.; the Court found the quotation does not appear in Abatie and could not be located via Westlaw/Google.

case_name: Nathan Strong v. The United States
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff quoted the MSPB decision as saying "such claims must be brought in another forum," but the quote does not appear in Strong v. Office of Special Counsel, No. DC-1221-23-0052-W-1, 2023 MSPB LEXIS 106; court independently verified the misquote. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed two statements to Stovall v. United States that do not appear in that opinion; court noted the statements are not in 71 Fed. Cl. 696 (2006). || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited a non-existent case, "Brands v. United States," which the court found to be fabricated. || Fabricated: Case Law | Government identified (and court independently verified) several other non-existent cases cited throughout plaintiff's filings (the government flagged eight such citations).

case_name: Robbin Y. Miller v. Andrew Stuart
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant cited 'Succession of Faget' as '838 So.2d 86 (La. App. 5 Cir. 2003)', but that citation does not appear and does not support the proposition she attributed to it; court noted the inaccuracy.

case_name: Richard LaRoche v. Darla Sterett (LaRoche)
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Brief included a purported quotation attributed to Sprague v. Nally that does not appear in the opinion; counsel admitted the language originated from an AI 'helper' and failed to verify it.
outcome: Order to file order in all pending Vermont Superior Court cases where Counsel appears

case_name: David J. Donovan v. Kathryn Thorson
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff's authorities cited incorrect appellate reporters or otherwise mis-cited case law; the court's review confirmed the citations were inaccurate and complicated appellate review. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed direct quotations to cited authorities that do not contain those quotations; the court found the quoted language absent from the cited sources.

case_name: Joshua Harris v. Pinnacle Bank
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law

case_name: Jeffery Todd Henson, Sr. v. Lynn A. Espejo
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited a non-existent case as authority; the Court identified the citation as nonexistent and observed the reporter page corresponds to United States v. Woodrum.

case_name: Kuigoua v. Park
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant cited "Demers v. City of Los Angeles, 42 Cal.App.3d 519, 522 (1974)," which respondents' counsel could not locate and the court found to be a non-existent citation. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Appellant repeatedly relied on Ladd v. Dart Equipment Corp., 230 Cal.App.3d 1088, 1097–1098 (1991) for a proposition the case does not support; the court found the citation misapplied.

case_name: Jacob Doe v. The University of North Carolina System
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited two cases that the Court found do not appear to exist. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff quoted material that does not exist in the cases purportedly quoted. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Court identified mischaracterizations of the holdings of cited cases in the Plaintiff's briefs. || Misrepresented: Case Law | An out-of-circuit case was misidentified in the briefs as a Fourth Circuit case.

case_name: United States v. Thomas Czartorski, et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Wright's brief cites a purported opinion 'United States v. Hang Le-Thy Tran, No. 3:07-CR-53, 2008 WL 2699394 (E.D. Ky. July 3, 2008)' which the court identified as non-existent. || Fabricated: Case Law | Brief cites 'United States v. Cope, 312 F. Supp. 2d 791 (E.D. Ky. 2004)' which the court treated as a fictitious opinion. || Fabricated: Case Law | Wright cites 'United States v. Abbott, 2023 WL 4106534 (E.D. Ky. June 27, 2023)'; the court found the citation non-existent and noted internal inconsistencies. || Misrepresented: Case Law | The brief quotes and characterizes United States v. Chavis, 296 F.3d 450 (6th Cir. 2002) in a manner the court found to misrepresent that Sixth Circuit holding. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Wright cites United States v. Tran, 433 F.3d 472, 478 (6th Cir. 2006) but the court found the holding as presented was misstated.
summary: In his response, Counsel acknowledged that he first researched relevant cases, and then "entered the cases into ChatGPT and requested that it highlight favorable arguments contained in the list of cases."

case_name: Shelton v. Parkland Health
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Brief contained multiple nonexistent legal citations generated by GAI; Court found the filings included 'nonexistent legal citations' and relied on opposing counsel's objections and investigation.

case_name: Kevin L. Swincher et al. v. Fay Servicing, LLC et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiffs cited a case the Court was unable to locate and deemed nonexistent. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiffs relied on Roche for the proposition that removal was used to obstruct discovery, but the Court found the case does not stand for that proposition. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiffs relied on Little for the obstruction proposition, but the Court found the case does not support their explanatory parenthetical.

case_name: Marques Johnson v. Capital One Financial Corporation
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Cohen v. Cap. One Funding, LLC and attributed a quotation—"Receivables are pooled and sold monthly."—to that opinion and misidentified the issuing court; the magistrate judge found the alleged quote does not appear in the Cohen decision and flagged it as likely an AI-generated hallucination.

case_name: Rivera Carrasquillo v. USA
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Counsel attributed a purported statement to Smith v. Phillips claiming the Court 'insisted' juror-bias inquiry cannot proceed without identifying the juror; the court found no such statement in Smith and called the citation misleading. || False Quotes: Case Law | Counsel included a non-existent quotation attributed to United States v. Paniagua-Ramos in support of limited disclosure of juror numerical identifiers; the court found Paniagua-Ramos does not address that subject and the quote was not present in the cited opinion.

case_name: Interest of M. O. W.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant's brief included multiple case citations that the court determined do not exist (apparently AI‑fabricated case law). The court expressly stated these cases 'do not exist.' || Misrepresented: Case Law | Appellant cited existing cases that the court found did not support the legal propositions he asserted (misrepresented precedent).

case_name: James Andrew Grimmer v. Citibank, N.A.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited this case in his opposition brief; the Court confirmed it does not exist and cited it as one of several nonexistent authorities identified by defendant. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited this authority in his opposition brief; the Court confirmed the citation is to a nonexistent decision. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff included this Third Circuit citation in his brief; the Court determined the opinion does not exist in reporters or databases. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited this D. Minn. opinion (Westlaw cite); the Court independently confirmed the decision cannot be located and is a fabricated citation.
summary: Plaintiff's opposition brief cited numerous nonexistent cases. Defendant identified the fabricated citations in its reply; plaintiff admitted reliance on an AI-based drafting tool and apologized. The court confirmed several cited cases do not exist but declined to pursue Rule 11 sanctions, finding apology, mitigation, and proportionality concerns.

case_name: Marc Henri David v. George Chiala Farms, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's counsel cited a nonexistent case 'Morgan v. State of California, 157 Cal. App. 4th 914, 922 (2007)'; the Court found no case of that name and noted the intended citation was Chamberlain v. Cocola Assocs., 958 F.2d 282 (9th Cir. 1992). || Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel for CDC cited a nonexistent authority 'Humphries v. Rice, 88 Cal. App. 4th 1413, 1424 (2001)'; the Court found the citation incorrect and noted counsel intended to cite Eddy v. Sharp, 199 Cal. App. 3d 858 (1988). || Misrepresented: Legal Norm | Plaintiff's counsel misquoted Cal. Civ. Code § 988(c), attributing language to the statute that it does not contain; the Court admonished counsel for the misquotation.
summary: The Court identified multiple instances where counsel cited nonexistent cases and misquoted Cal. Civ. Code § 988(c). The Court admonished counsel, noted corrections in later briefing, and declined to credit the arguments based on the erroneous citations. No sanctions were imposed.

case_name: Habib Miah v. Morgan Stanley & Co. International PLC, et al.
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Defendants identified quotations in the filings that do not appear in the authorities cited; court observed these apparent false quotations as indicative of AI use. || Fabricated: Case Law | Defendants identified a cited decision purportedly from the Eastern District of New York that does not exist; the court noted this as apparent AI-produced fabrication.

case_name: Travis C. Mills et al. v. Rocket Mortgage LLC et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Court found multiple citations to cases that do not exist in plaintiffs' filings, likely generated by GenAI. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Court identified mischaracterizations of existing cases and incorrect citations in plaintiffs' submissions.

case_name: Jamison Warfield v. W.N. Morehouse Truck Line, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Morehouse pointed out that Warfield's briefs relied on incorrect and nonexistent case citations; the Court acknowledged the allegation but did not analyze specific fabricated citations.
summary: Morehouse replied that Warfield's filings relied on incorrect and nonexistent case citations; the Court noted that allegation but did not impose sanctions and dismissed the case for lack of personal jurisdiction.
